title: hypericum quadrangulum subsp. dubium
intro: Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. dubium is a lesser-known subspecies of St. John's Wort, a flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and opposite, two-ranked leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. dubium include its potential to treat anxiety, depression, and other mental health conditions, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
